description: Change the smallest runtime entry and check bundler resolution before moving shared code.
body: Use when browser bundles, CLI bundles, or library build outputs are affected. Do not move Node-only dependencies into shared modules consumed by browser entries. Inspect import graph, package exports, sideEffects hints, and build config before edit. Verify with the project build or a bundle smoke that changed entrypoints compile. Risk boundary includes accidental polyfills, bundle bloat, and runtime-only failures hidden by unit tests. Use when JavaScript Runtime and Tooling work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
